title: Zyox
subtitle: Catering & Smart Food Ordering App
description: A premium catering and smart food ordering application featuring interactive chef logs and customized menu planner dashboards.
problem: Traditional online catering systems are rigid, causing massive communication gaps between clients and chefs. This results in inaccurate order quantities, mismatched dietary requirements, and logistical chaos during event execution.
solution: Designed a real-time chef communication interface with interactive cooking logs and a dynamic menu planner. The platform automatically calculates portion scaling, translates custom dietary restrictions into ingredient checklists, and provides instant kitchen telemetry updates.

title: Montra Connect
subtitle: EV Smart Rickshaw Fleet
description: Interactive telemetry and operations dashboard for the modern Montra electric three-wheeler auto rickshaws.
problem: Rickshaw fleet operators lacked live tracking, battery life predictions, and route optimization. Fleet downtime was high due to late detection of vehicle failures and unmonitored battery degradation.
solution: Developed an IoT-enabled operations dashboard powered by real-time WebSockets. The system streams telemetry data from EV microcontrollers, visualizes active routes on dynamic maps, predicts remaining battery range based on driving patterns, and flags anomaly alerts instantly.

title: iSportzz
subtitle: Sports Social Network App
description: A gamified sports social media app connecting athletes, matching game brackets, and hosting selfie-backed live logs.
problem: Amateur athletes struggled to find players of similar skill levels locally, resulting in poorly balanced matches. Existing platforms lacked a reliable way to verify game results, leading to inflated ratings and toxic competition.
solution: Engineered a gamified sports social hub with a skill-based matching algorithm and automated tournament brackets. Designed a peer-verified match log system where players upload game-end selfies, using community consensus to update ratings and maintain fair play.

title: Nova Terminal
subtitle: Gamified SSH Dashboard
description: A Sci-Fi themed command-line interface featuring interactive cosmic shortcuts and mascot sidekick animations.
problem: Managing complex remote servers and Docker containers via raw SSH is dry, visually unintuitive, and slow. Command errors often lack descriptive context, increasing troubleshooting time for junior developers.
solution: Created a rich terminal dashboard rendering live output via Xterm.js. Embedded cosmic shortcut nodes that execute custom scripts on drag-and-drop. Built an interactive AI-powered mascot sidekick that reacts visually to terminal statuses and provides contextual command debugging.

title: Solaris Auth
subtitle: Cosmic Security Gateway
description: Multi-factor authentication visualizer using orbits and solar flare heat maps to block intrusion paths.
problem: Standard identity verification gateways feel tedious and opaque. Users skip complex security measures because they don't understand the underlying threat landscape or how access routes are being verified.
solution: Visualized authentication as a cosmic orbital network where security challenge layers act as planetary rings. Integrated Redis-backed rate limiting and geo-location tracking mapped as solar flares. High-risk attempts trigger gravitational collapses that lock login ports.

title: Starlight CDN
subtitle: Global Edge Router
description: Geographic traffic routing system visualized as star paths distributing code payloads across satellites.
problem: Traditional Content Delivery Networks route traffic based on static, coarse location databases. This causes high latency spikes and failure propagation when local edge nodes experience sudden bandwidth bottlenecks.
solution: Architected a routing visualizer rendering geographic packet flow as light beams traveling between satellites. Implemented custom edge routers in Rust compiled to WebAssembly running on Cloudflare Workers, automatically diverting traffic along low-latency pathways.
